behavior_summary: Handles the player's 'U8P_MIR::look' (examine) action for this object/NPC: when examined it displays a short on-screen bark containing the text 'mirror' and then suspends the process.
No branching, parameter use, or global flag interaction is present; the routine is a short, linear handler that provides a single descriptive label.
The association with a mirror (or an NPC id 'MIR') is inferred from the bark text and file naming, not from explicit state or flags in the script.
notable_control_flow: U8P_MIR::look executes a simple linear sequence: call Bark('mirror') -> call suspend().
There are no conditionals, loops, or additional calls; execution yields/stops immediately after the bark.
open_questions: Is U8P_MIR attached to an in-game object named 'mirror' or an NPC whose id is 'MIR'? (inferred from bark text; confidence: medium)
Does suspend() here permanently terminate the U8P_MIR::look process or temporarily yield to the engine? (behavior inferred; confidence: medium)
Are there longer descriptive texts elsewhere (e.g., a 'read' or 'examine long' handler) associated with this object that are not present in this snippet? (confidence: low)
